summary: DHH argues that Apple's MacBook keyboard failure rate is far higher than Apple acknowledges, because most affected users never contact Apple for repairs. He presents data from Basecamp showing 30% of MacBook users currently have keyboard issues, and a Twitter poll of 7,000+ respondents showing a 63% failure rate. He notes the 3rd generation membrane keyboard fix didn't solve the problem, with nearly half of 2018+ MacBooks at Basecamp having failed keyboards. He frames this as a disaster that threatens decades of Apple's brand goodwill.
key_points: Apple's official failure statistics are misleading because most users never report keyboard problems, instead living with broken keys or switching to external keyboards
30% of MacBook users at Basecamp are currently dealing with keyboard issues, and the 3rd generation membrane keyboard made things worse, not better
A Twitter poll of 7,000+ respondents showed 63% failure rate, with only 11% of affected users actually contacting Apple
The real test of Apple's health is not the keyboard crisis itself but whether they accept responsibility and respond appropriately
money_quotes: Apple keep insisting that only a 'small number of customers have problems' with the MacBook keyboards. That's bollocks.
The fact is that many people simply do not contact Apple when their MacBook keyboards fail. They just live with an S key that stutters or a spacebar that intermittently gives double.
Out of the 47 people using MacBooks at the company, a staggering 30% are dealing with keyboard issues right now!!

Worth noting here is that the 3rd generation membrane keyboard did nothing to fix the issues. Six out of thirteen – nearly half!! – of the 2018+ MacBooks we have at the company have a failed keyboard.
This is a disaster. A complete unmitigated disaster.
In a time of crisis, the event itself is less indicative of the health of a company than the response.
Is Apple going to accept that they're currently alienating and undermining decades of goodwill by shipping broken computers in mass quantities?
key_insight: Apple's reliance on support contact data to measure keyboard failure rates creates a massive blind spot, as most users simply endure broken keyboards rather than seeking repair.
